result.summary: Sanity exposes a tenant-scoped HTTP API, a deployed GraphQL API, a hosted MCP server at https://mcp.sanity.io, a CLI, and a separate Content Agent API; authentication is primarily via Sanity API tokens, CLI login sessions, and MCP OAuth.
result.description: Sanity is a content platform built around its Content Lake datastore and customizable Studio. It provides APIs and tooling for querying, updating, and managing content, plus AI-oriented products such as a hosted MCP server and Content Agent integrations.

sanity_api_token.label: Sanity project API token
sanity_api_token.setup: In [Sanity Manage](https://sanity.io/manage), open your project, then go to **API** → **Tokens** and create a project-level token. Choose the minimum role needed; for example, the Content Agent API docs require an **Editor** role or above for write operations.

sanity_cli_session.label: Sanity CLI logged-in session
sanity_cli_session.setup: Install the CLI and run `sanity login`. The CLI signs you in through a browser-based OAuth flow and stores the resulting session token locally on your machine, as described in [CLI authentication](https://www.sanity.io/docs/apis-and-sdks/cli-authentication).

sanity_mcp_oauth.label: Sanity MCP OAuth authorization
sanity_mcp_oauth.setup: Connect an MCP client to `https://mcp.sanity.io` and authenticate with the server's default OAuth flow. Sanity's MCP docs state the hosted server supports OAuth by default; for example Claude Code can be configured with `claude mcp add Sanity -t http https://mcp.sanity.io --scope user` and then prompts you to authorize access.

sanity_org_id.label: Sanity organization ID
sanity_org_id.setup: For Content Agent integrations, get your organization ID from your Sanity project settings; the docs say it is visible in your project settings. Use it together with a project API token when configuring the `content-agent` provider.
